description: Connect to remote machines via SSH and execute commands. Manages persistent SSH sessions through a local gateway service. Supports password and private key authentication.

conditional_requirements: Do NOT call remote_shell actions directly — the WASM sandbox blocks all HTTP to localhost. Use the built-in shell tool with curl to talk to the gateway at http://127.0.0.1:9022. See SKILL.md for ready-to-paste curl commands.
connect (via curl POST /connect): requires host, username, auth, and either host_key_fingerprint (SHA256:...) or insecure_ignore_host_key: true.
execute (via curl POST /execute): requires session_id and command. timeout_secs (1-3600, default 30) is optional.
list sessions (via curl GET /sessions): no additional requirements.
health (via curl GET /health): no additional requirements; probes the local gateway.
notes: WARNING: The IronClaw WASM sandbox blocks all HTTP to 127.0.0.1 — every remote_shell action fails with a sandbox restriction error. Use the built-in shell tool with curl instead. See SKILL.md for ready-to-paste curl commands for every action.
The SSH gateway service (`remote-shell-gateway`) must be running locally. Default port is 9022; override with `gateway_port`.

Sessions persist until explicitly disconnected, the gateway TTL elapses (default 3600s), or the gateway is restarted (all sessions are in-memory). Reuse `session_id` across many commands — do NOT reconnect for every command.
Stdout / stderr are capped at 10 MB each; output beyond that is truncated.
Use `tool_info(name: "remote_shell", detail: "schema")` for the full action schema.

auth.instructions: The remote-shell-gateway service must be running. If you configured a bearer token for the gateway, enter it here. Otherwise, leave empty or set any value.
auth.token_hint: Optional bearer token for the gateway API

required_secrets.name: ssh_gateway_token
required_secrets.prompt: SSH gateway bearer token (optional, press Enter to skip)
